source_jobs.title: Senior Data Analyst
source_jobs.description:
DAVE VS. GOLIATH. WE’RE DAVE.

Dave is a financial app on a mission to build products that level the financial playing field. It is redefining the financial landscape by leveraging technology to create an affordable, transparent, and user-centric access to liquidity for millions of Americans. As a leading innovator in the U.S. financial services sector, Dave’s digital financial platform offers products designed to meet the credit needs of those underserved by traditional financial institutions. Dave’s offerings include its flagship ExtraCash product, providing members up to $500 within minutes. The company is on track to launch several new product offerings in 2026, including a Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) option.



Dave is focused on serving Americans who are financially vulnerable or living paycheck to paycheck. Dave is leading the charge in creating a new era of credit products that prioritizes speed, affordability, and accessibility, making it the go-to financial partner for those who need it most.



At Dave, data is the foundation of the financial infrastructure we build for millions of members. As our first Senior Data Analyst, you'll establish the reconciliation foundation for Dave's data systems — designing the pipelines, anomaly rules, and AI-powered matching logic that keep our financial data trustworthy and audit-ready. You'll sit at the intersection of data engineering, finance, and operations, serving as the technical lead who helps Engineering, Finance/Accounting, and Reconciliation Operations move faster and catch problems before they become incidents.

This role is for someone who thinks in systems, is energized by financial data complexity, and wants to build something foundational from the ground up.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build the reconciliation foundation. Design and implement the data infrastructure that reconciles transactions across internal systems (e.g.,operation, ledger) and external partners (ACH networks, banking rails, payment processors), ensuring every dollar in equals every dollar out.

 - Lead anomaly detection and rules development. Develop and maintain a library of anomaly detection rules — covering timing differences, partial matches, unexpected delays, and true errors — and evolve them over time using ML-based signals as data matures.

 - Implement AI-powered matching. Apply probabilistic and fuzzy matching techniques to automatically resolve ambiguous or near-match transactions, reducing manual review queues and improving straight-through processing rates.

 - Flag and triage unmatched records. Build real-time and batch pipelines that surface unmatched, delayed, or anomalous transactions to the right stakeholders, with enough context for fast resolution.

 - Partner cross-functionally as the technical lead. Serve as the primary data partner for Engineering (system integration and pipeline reliability), Finance/Accounting (period-end close, audit support), and Recon Operations (workflow tooling, exception handling).

 - Define data quality standards. Establish validation frameworks that distinguish expected timing lags from genuine discrepancies, and document data contracts between upstream systems.

 - Drive automation and process improvement. Identify and eliminate manual reconciliation workloads through scalable pipelines, alerting systems, and self-serve tooling for operations teams.

 - Elevate the team. Share reconciliation and data quality best practices, mentor analysts, and help establish this function as a core capability within the Analytics org.




WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - 5+ years of experience in data analytics, data engineering, or financial data roles — ideally with direct exposure to reconciliation, settlements, or financial operations.

 - Deep SQL proficiency and strong Python skills (pandas, PySpark, or equivalent); experience with DBT a plus.

 - Hands-on experience with Snowflake and/or Databricks, including pipeline development, scheduling, and data modeling.

 - Familiar

source_jobs.title: Lead Machine Learning Scientist
source_jobs.description:
DAVE VS. GOLIATH. WE’RE DAVE.

Dave is a financial app on a mission to build products that level the financial playing field. It is redefining the financial landscape by leveraging technology to create an affordable, transparent, and user-centric access to liquidity for millions of Americans. As a leading innovator in the U.S. financial services sector, Dave’s digital financial platform offers products designed to meet the credit needs of those underserved by traditional financial institutions. Dave’s offerings include its flagship ExtraCash product, providing members up to $500 within minutes. The company is on track to launch several new product offerings in 2026, including a Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) option.



Dave is focused on serving Americans who are financially vulnerable or living paycheck to paycheck. Dave is leading the charge in creating a new era of credit products that prioritizes speed, affordability, and accessibility, making it the go-to financial partner for those who need it most.



We’re looking for a Lead Machine Learning Scientist to own and scale ML-driven Marketing/Growth/Product capabilities at Dave. This role will drive how we use data and machine learning to improve acquisition, engagement, retention, and monetization across a multi-product ecosystem.

You will help identify high-impact opportunities, building production-grade models, and shaping the roadmap for ML in Marketing/Growth. This is a highly strategic and hands-on role requiring both technical depth and business acumen.



ML Strategy for Marketing/Growth

 - Architect and lead machine learning solutions across teams, driving multi-person, cross-functional initiatives

 - Define and execute the roadmap for applying ML to improve marketing efficiency and growth

 - Proactively identify high-impact opportunities where ML can drive step-function improvements (not just respond to requests)

 - Partner closely with Marketing, Product, and Finance to align ML investments with business priorities
   
   

Build High-Impact Models

 - Lead development and deployment of core models, including:
   
   - Propensity (conversion, engagement)
   
   - Churn prevention and intervention
   
   - Customer Lifetime Value (LTV) models, co-develop with Finance to align on assumptions, definitions, and business use cases
   
   - Cross-sell / next-best-action models across Dave’s products

 - Improve onboarding, targeting, personalization, and segmentation at scale

 - Work across modeling lifecycle from problem formulation, training, calibration, and iteration in production

 - Build agentic engineering workflows that accelerate development, testing, and documentation
   
   

Data & Platform Leverage

 - Analyze large and complex dataset; Identify and evaluate high-leverage internal and external data sources to improve model performance

 - Build business cases for new data acquisition and lead onboarding efforts

 - Ensure models are scalable, measurable, and tightly integrated into marketing workflows
   
   

Optimization & Efficiency

 - Continuously evaluate and improve marketing spend efficiency through ML-driven insights and models

 - Identify and resolve cost inefficiencies across models and pipelines

 - Design and optimize reward and incentive strategies (e.g., referral incentives, promotional offers) to maximize user acquisition, activation, and retention while managing cost efficiency

 - Develop models to determine optimal reward levels and targeting, balancing conversion lift with incremental cost to improve ROI

 - Evaluate and measure incrementality of rewards and promotions using experimentation (A/B testing, uplift modeling) to ensure incentives drive true behavioral change rather than subsidizing existing demand
   
   

Technical Excellence

 - Set standards for model development, experimentation, and validation

 - Promote adoption of modern ML techniques, leverage pre-trained models, including effective use of AI/LLMs where applicable

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager, Flex
source_jobs.description:
DAVE VS. GOLIATH. WE’RE DAVE.

Dave is a financial app on a mission to build products that level the financial playing field. It is redefining the financial landscape by leveraging technology to create an affordable, transparent, and user-centric access to liquidity for millions of Americans. As a leading innovator in the U.S. financial services sector, Dave’s digital financial platform offers products designed to meet the credit needs of those underserved by traditional financial institutions. Dave’s offerings include its flagship ExtraCash product, providing members up to $500 within minutes. The company is on track to launch several new product offerings in 2026, including a Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) option.



Dave is focused on serving Americans who are financially vulnerable or living paycheck to paycheck. Dave is leading the charge in creating a new era of credit products that prioritizes speed, affordability, and accessibility, making it the go-to financial partner for those who need it most.



Flex card is Dave's newest and most ambitious product, a pay-in-4 card built for the millions of members living paycheck to paycheck who've never had fair access to credit. It's not a feature. It's a new business line, and it's just getting started.


This role sits at the center of Flex card's growth and adoption. You'll own meaningful parts of the roadmap from onboarding conversion to activation, engagement, and retention, turning member insights and performance data into features that scale. You'll work closely with Engineering, Design, Credit, Underwriting, Legal, and Operations to improve access, drive usage, and build a card that our members genuinely keep top-of-wallet.



If you like real ownership, innovative financial products, and building for people who are genuinely underserved, this role is for you.



WHAT YOU’LL BUILD & OWN

 - Drive Flex card adoption: Lead Flex card features from discovery through launch and iteration, spanning onboarding, activation, spend, repayment, and member communication

 - Ship with speed and conviction: Lead your squad through discovery, delivery, and in-market optimization - ensuring fast execution without losing sight of long-term impact.

 - Own the numbers: Drive core metrics including offer-to-activation conversion, card usage, repayment behavior, and member retention

 - Enable flawless execution: Translate goals into clear, prioritized backlogs in partnership with product, research, design, engineering, and data teams, leading to fast, high-quality execution

 - Lead with evidence: Use qualitative research and quantitative data to surface the highest-impact opportunities across the member funnel

 - Champion Flex card evolution: Explore and validate how Flex card evolves as a multi-product experience alongside ExtraCash and Dave Banking




THE IMPACT

Flex card is Dave's bet on becoming top-of-wallet for our members – not just a safety net, but the card they reach for every day. It's cashflow-based, no-credit-check, and built to give members spending power without the traps that come with traditional credit.

In this role, you'll help shape a product in its earliest and most formative stages, making decisions that directly affect member access, financial outcomes, and how Dave grows. The problems are real, the stakes are high, and your fingerprints will be all over it.



WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Experience

 - 5+ years of product management experience including 2+ years in fintech, lending, cards, or related consumer financial products

 - Proven track record of improving growth, conversion, and retention in consumer products

 - Proven ability to manage a complex product surface area with many stakeholders, using strong product judgment to balance user experience, risk, and business outcomes

 - Experience working closely with senior leaders to propose, refine, and ship new initiatives

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Comfortable using data to inform decisions

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager, Growth Acquisition
source_jobs.description:
DAVE VS. GOLIATH. WE’RE DAVE.

Dave is a financial app on a mission to build products that level the financial playing field. It is redefining the financial landscape by leveraging technology to create an affordable, transparent, and user-centric access to liquidity for millions of Americans. As a leading innovator in the U.S. financial services sector, Dave’s digital financial platform offers products designed to meet the credit needs of those underserved by traditional financial institutions. Dave’s offerings include its flagship ExtraCash product, providing members up to $500 within minutes. The company is on track to launch several new product offerings in 2026, including a Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) option.



Dave is focused on serving Americans who are financially vulnerable or living paycheck to paycheck. Dave is leading the charge in creating a new era of credit products that prioritizes speed, affordability, and accessibility, making it the go-to financial partner for those who need it most.



We’re looking for a Senior Product Manager, Growth Acquisition, to lead the product strategy, implementation, and scaling of our Marketing Technology ecosystem.



THE OPPORTUNITY

This role is responsible for the end-to-end growth foundation that enables efficient, measurable, and compliant user acquisition, cross-sell, and retention across web and app. You’ll lead the roadmap for attribution, tracking, experimentation, and marketing integrations. It requires strong technical fluency and deep domain expertise in the Marketing Technology landscape. You will be the owner of the tech foundation that allows us to efficiently acquire, cross-sell, and retain users across web and app platforms.

This is a deeply cross-functional, product-led role at the intersection of product, marketing, data, and engineering. Success requires strong technical fluency, clear product judgment, and the ability to balance near-term growth needs with long-term platform health in a regulated fintech environment.



WHAT YOU'LL BUILD & OWN

 - Set the vision, deliver the results: Own and define Dave’s MarTech Stack, including paid channel automation (SEM, Paid Social, Programmatic, etc), attribution, lifecycle, and experimentation infrastructure. Make build vs buy decisions, including vendor selection, RFP process, and integration.

 - Ship with speed and conviction: Own end-to-end enablement of web growth strategy — from CMS, landing pages, and experimentation to web-to-app and deep-linking — while partnering with core product teams to streamline registration, onboarding, and the full paid media-to-app customer journey.

 - Enable flawless execution: Act as the connective tissue across Engineering, Data, and Marketing — ensuring alignment on priorities, clarity on plans, and efficient execution toward business outcomes.

 - Build a culture of evidence: Drive rapid web experimentation by building and maintaining experimentation frameworks and infrastructure to test new growth hypotheses, from channel to creative to landing experiences.

 - Stay ahead of MarTech and AdTech evolution: Drive the best-in-class playbooks and stay ahead of privacy changes to platform and policy shifts, as well as new entrants and new technologies — translating risks, impacts, and opportunities into clear guidance.


THE IMPACT

Your work defines how efficiently we grow. You’ll build the systems that connect marketing investment to real member outcomes - helping Dave scale responsibly while delivering better, more relevant experiences.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years of experience in MarTech and Product Management in a high-volume B2C technology-driven environment (fintech experience is a plus).

 - Deep MarTech domain expertise with hands-on experience across analytics and attribution platforms (AppsFlyer, Braze, Firebase, Google Analytics, Amplitude, or similar), including a strong command of iOS tracking and measurement frameworks such as SKAdNetwork (SKAN).

 - Cross-

source_jobs.title: Sr. IT Systems Engineer, AI & Business Solutions
source_jobs.description:
DAVE VS. GOLIATH. WE’RE DAVE.

Dave is a financial app on a mission to build products that level the financial playing field. It is redefining the financial landscape by leveraging technology to create an affordable, transparent, and user-centric access to liquidity for millions of Americans. As a leading innovator in the U.S. financial services sector, Dave’s digital financial platform offers products designed to meet the credit needs of those underserved by traditional financial institutions. Dave’s offerings include its flagship ExtraCash product, providing members up to $500 within minutes. The company is on track to launch several new product offerings in 2026, including a Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) option.



Dave is focused on serving Americans who are financially vulnerable or living paycheck to paycheck. Dave is leading the charge in creating a new era of credit products that prioritizes speed, affordability, and accessibility, making it the go-to financial partner for those who need it most.



We’re hiring a Senior IT Systems Engineer to help design and scale how we manage compliance, security, and audit readiness at Dave. This role focuses on building systems and automation that make compliance continuous, reliable, and embedded into how we operate.




THE OPPORTUNITY

You’ll operate at the intersection of IT, security, and compliance, and you’ll be an influential contributor for a team that helps design systems to replace manual audit work with automated, scalable solutions. This includes automating evidence collection, strengthening IT General Controls, and integrating tools so compliance becomes part of day-to-day workflows rather than a periodic exercise.

This is a high-impact role with real ownership. You’ll help shape how we scale our internal process through better systems, working across teams to turn requirements into durable, low-friction solutions.




WHAT YOU’LL BUILD & OWN

 - Automate or optimize high-volume manual processes (audit evidence collection, data gathering, compliance reporting) using APIs and workflows to reduce manual effort and human error

 - Build integrations that pull data from systems of record (Okta, Workspace, GitHub, Security tools, Google Cloud, Snowflake) and consolidate it in BigQuery for audit readiness and analytics

 - Design and implement agentic workflows that allow non-engineering stakeholders to interact with company data and reports through conversational interfaces, reducing their need to manually query or compile information

 - Own the operational lifecycle of integrations: monitoring, alerting, troubleshooting, and evolving them as business requirements change

 - Identify manual handoffs and repetitive tasks across IT, audit, and business functions, then build automation or agent-driven solutions to eliminate them

 - Work cross-functionally with business teams to understand pain points, translate them into technical solutions, and measure the impact on time savings and compliance readiness

 - Build scalable, resilient, and secure systems
   
   


THE IMPACT

Your work will enable Dave to scale securely with greater confidence, reducing audit overhead while increasing trust in our systems and controls.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Experience & Technical Foundation

 - Bachelor's degree (or equivalent four-year degree) required

 - 5–7 years of experience in IT systems engineering, automation, internal web services or a related technical role

 - Proficiency in scripting or programming (e.g., Python) for building integrations and automation

 - Experience building agents and end-to-end automation across systems using APIs, scripting, or workflow platforms

 - Hands-on experience with GCP (Compute Engine, Cloud Functions, Cloud Run, BigQuery, Cloud Storage) and understanding infrastructure as code (e.g. terraform)

 - Hands-on experience with GitHub or equivalent version control, including CI/CD automation (GitHub Actions, automated testing, deployments)

 - Stron

source_jobs.title: Website Strategy & Experience Lead
source_jobs.description:
DAVE VS. GOLIATH. WE’RE DAVE.

Dave is a financial app on a mission to build products that level the financial playing field. It is redefining the financial landscape by leveraging technology to create an affordable, transparent, and user-centric access to liquidity for millions of Americans. As a leading innovator in the U.S. financial services sector, Dave’s digital financial platform offers products designed to meet the credit needs of those underserved by traditional financial institutions. Dave’s offerings include its flagship ExtraCash product, providing members up to $500 within minutes. The company is on track to launch several new product offerings in 2026, including a Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) option.



Dave is focused on serving Americans who are financially vulnerable or living paycheck to paycheck. Dave is leading the charge in creating a new era of credit products that prioritizes speed, affordability, and accessibility, making it the go-to financial partner for those who need it most.



We’re hiring a Website Strategy & Experience Lead to own and evolve Dave’s website as a critical growth channel.

This role sits within the User Acquisition team and is responsible for shaping how our website attracts, educates, and converts potential members. You will define the strategy for how our site supports discovery, builds trust, and moves users from web to app.

You’ll work closely with product, engineering, brand, and analytics partners to build a scalable web platform that supports experimentation, improves conversion, and strengthens Dave’s presence across search and emerging AI discovery surfaces.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - Define and evolve the long-term strategy for Dave’s website as an acquisition and discovery platform, connecting SEO, content, UX, and experimentation into a unified roadmap.

 - Analyze website performance, user journeys, and conversion funnels to identify opportunities that improve acquisition efficiency and web-to-app conversion.

 - Partner with engineering and product to develop a flexible website architecture in Contentful that supports scalable landing pages, experimentation, and rapid iteration.

 - Lead search and discovery strategy across technical SEO, site architecture, and content frameworks, helping Dave appear in traditional search as well as emerging AI and generative discovery experiences.

 - Build and manage an experimentation roadmap using tools like Ninetailed, translating test results into scalable improvements across the website.

 - Collaborate with brand and content teams to develop educational and product-focused content that strengthens trust and positions Dave as a credible financial resource.

 - Ensure website experiences meet accessibility and regulatory standards, including ADA and CCPA requirements.


THE IMPACT

Your work will shape one of the first experiences millions of people have with Dave. A well-designed and discoverable website helps people understand their financial options and confidently take the next step toward becoming a member.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Experience & Technical Foundation

 - 8+ years of experience in website strategy, growth marketing, or digital experience management (specifically for consumer products/b2c).

 - Proven track record owning a high-traffic website that directly contributes to user acquisition and CAC efficiency.

 - Experience partnering with product and engineering teams to build scalable web infrastructure and experimentation frameworks.

 - Strong analytical skills and experience using tools like Google Analytics to evaluate performance and guide decisions.

 - Experience working with modern CMS platforms such as Contentful and experimentation tools like Ninetailed.

 - Deep understanding of SEO, conversion rate optimization, and modern discovery channels, including AI and generative search.

 - Strong cross-functional collaboration skills across marketing, product, engineering, design, and legal partners.

Bonus

source_jobs.title: Manager, Software Engineering (Front End Infrastructure)
source_jobs.description:
DAVE VS. GOLIATH. WE’RE DAVE.

Dave is a financial app on a mission to build products that level the financial playing field. It is redefining the financial landscape by leveraging technology to create an affordable, transparent, and user-centric access to liquidity for millions of Americans. As a leading innovator in the U.S. financial services sector, Dave’s digital financial platform offers products designed to meet the credit needs of those underserved by traditional financial institutions. Dave’s offerings include its flagship ExtraCash product, providing members up to $500 within minutes. The company is on track to launch several new product offerings in 2026, including a Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) option.



Dave is focused on serving Americans who are financially vulnerable or living paycheck to paycheck. Dave is leading the charge in creating a new era of credit products that prioritizes speed, affordability, and accessibility, making it the go-to financial partner for those who need it most.



About the team and role:

Dave is looking for an experienced and motivated Engineering Manager to join our Front End Infrastructure (FEI) team. As an Engineering Manager at Dave, you will play an instrumental role in leading the squad responsible for the foundational systems that every mobile engineer at Dave relies on, from CI/CD pipelines and shared libraries to mobile observability, product analytics, experimentation platform, design systems, and release tooling. You'll be responsible for fostering a collaborative and innovative environment, overseeing project delivery, and driving excellence across the entire mobile engineering organization. This role reports to the Director, DevX and Infrastructure Engineering.



What you’ll do:

 - Balance engineering leadership and people management with hands-on technical engagement, maintaining sufficient depth in the codebase and platform architecture to guide technical strategy, influence architectural decisions, and mentor engineers.

 - Bring forward creative technical solutions to handle complex frontend platform challenges by collaborating with product engineering, design, marketing, and other stakeholders.

 - Stay close enough to the code to guide architectural decisions, review mobile platform work, and provide technical direction across React Native, iOS, Android, and our CI/CD and developer tooling stack.

 - Hire, lead, mentor, and motivate talented engineers and help them grow professionally.

 - Collaborate with business and engineering stakeholders to come up with the roadmap, vision, and purpose for the mobile platform.

 - Facilitate and contribute to conversations with Lead and Staff Engineers to come up with scalable mobile architecture, better engineering practices, and prioritize tech debt.

 - Communicate the purpose and "why" behind the current and future priorities to the squad and stakeholders.

 - Remove roadblocks for team members, both technical and otherwise.

 - Be the bridge between the squad and the rest of the engineering org and leadership by making sure that information flows both ways.

 - Be a spokesperson and champion for changes initiated by peers, managers, and leadership.

 - Co-own and measure metrics to enable data-driven decision-making around platform health and developer experience (educated prioritization of tech debt, tooling investments, and developer productivity) in collaboration with engineering stakeholders. And take the lead on solutions to improve when needed.

 - Work closely with peer Engineering Managers and their teams to prioritize and balance platform investments with squad-specific feature needs.

 - Establish processes that improve the mobile design-to-development workflow, partnering closely with design and product to ensure seamless collaboration on shared UI patterns and components.



What you’ll need:

 - 6+ years of software development experience.

 - 1.5+ years of engineering management experience.

 - Hands-on expe

notes: Financial app with a strong tech core, multiple engineering and product roles
